parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Optional. The maximum number of assets to return.

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description: Optional. A pagination token returned from a previous call.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description:
Optional. An expression for filtering the results.
Filterable fields:

+ `state`
+ `mediaType`
+ `mimeType`
+ `displayName`
+ `path`
+ `creator`
+ `createTime` (supports comparison operators)
+ `expireTime` (supports comparison operators)

Examples:

+ `state = ACTIVE` — only active assets.
+ `mediaType = VIDEO` — only video assets.
+ `path = "/sports/*"` — assets under the sports path.

For more information, see [AIP-160](https://aip.dev/160).

parameters.name: orderBy
parameters.description:
Optional. A comma-separated list of fields to order by.
Supported fields:

+ `displayName`
+ `createTime`
+ `updateTime`
+ `sizeBytes`
+ `name`

Example: `createTime desc, displayName`

parameters.name: showDeleted
parameters.description: Optional. If true, include soft-deleted assets in the response.

post.summary:
Creates a new asset in the specified project. The asset undergoes
a full ingestion pipeline as a long-running operation including
format validation, metadata extraction, thumbnail generation,
and AI processing.
post.description:
If no endpoint or filename is provided, a PLACEHOLDER asset is
created for the Request workflow.

The caller must have `assets.assets.create` permission on the parent
project.

parameters.name: asset
parameters.description:
Required. The asset to create. Must include display_name. If no
endpoint/filename/size_bytes are provided, a PLACEHOLDER asset is
created (no file upload, used by the Request workflow).

parameters.name: endpoint
parameters.description:
Optional. The storage endpoint to store the asset on. Format:
`organizations/{organization}/storageGateways/{gateway}/endpoints/{endpoint}`

Required for file uploads. Omit when creating a PLACEHOLDER asset
(the endpoint is set when the asset is fulfilled).

parameters.name: filename
parameters.description:
Optional. The original filename from the client. Used to determine
media type if magic bytes are ambiguous.

parameters.name: sizeBytes
parameters.description:
Optional. The file size in bytes. Required for multipart uploads
so the server can compute part boundaries.

parameters.name: validateOnly
parameters.description:
Optional. If set to true, the request will only validate the
request without persisting it.

get.summary: Retrieves an asset by resource name.

delete.summary:
Soft-deletes an asset. The asset enters DELETE_REQUESTED state and
will be purged after the retention period. Can be restored with
UndeleteAsset before purge.

parameters.name: etag
parameters.description: Optional. The etag for optimistic concurrency.

parameters.name: force
parameters.description: Optional. If true, delete the asset even if it has versions.

parameters.name: validateOnly
parameters.description: Optional. If set to true, validate only without persisting.

patch.summary:
Updates an asset's mutable fields (display_name, path, annotations).
File content is immutable — create a new version instead.

parameters.name: asset
parameters.description: Required. The asset with updated fields.

displayName.description: Required. A human-readable name for the asset.

state.description: Output only. The current lifecycle state.

mediaType.description:
Output only. The broad media category, determined during ingestion
from the file's MIME type.

mimeType.description:
Output only. The MIME type of the original file (e.g. `image/jpeg`,
`video/mp4`). Determined from magic bytes during ingestion, not
from the file extension.

path.description:
Optional. An organizational path for display grouping (e.g.
`/sports/highlights/2026/`). Not enforced as a hierarchy — purely
for UI presentation and filesystem import preservation. Use tags
for structured categorization.

endpoint.title:
Output only. The storage endpoint where the asset's files are stored.
Format: `organizations/{organization}/storageGateways/{gateway}/endpoints/{endpoint}`

checksumSha256.description:
Output only. SHA-256 checksum of the original file. Used for
deduplication — if a file with the same checksum already exists
in the project, the existing asset is returned instead of creating
a duplicate.

sizeBytes.description: Output only. Size of the original file in bytes.

latestVersion.description: Output only. The latest version of the asset.

versionCount.description: Output only. Total number of versions.

technicalMetadata.description:
Output only. Technical metadata extracted from the file (EXIF, XMP).
Junk metadata (Adobe comments, software tags) is stripped.
Stored as a structured JSON object — fields vary by media type.

aiDescription.description:
Output only. AI-generated description of the asset's visual or
audio content. Used for semantic search.

transcription.description:
Output only. Transcription of audio/video content. Indexed for
full-text search.

duration.description: Output only. Duration of the media, for video and audio assets.

width.description: Output only. Width in pixels, for image and video assets.

height.description: Output only. Height in pixels, for image and video assets.

expireTime.description:
Optional. The time at which this asset should expire. When set,
a background reaper will soft-delete the asset after this time.
Can be set directly or via a retention policy (tag-based rules).

ttl.description:
Input only. The time-to-live for the asset, as an alternative to
setting expire_time directly. The server computes expire_time from
create_time + ttl.

annotations.description: Optional. Annotations associated with this asset.

etag.description:
Output only. A checksum computed by the server based on the current
value of the Asset resource.

creator.description: Output only. The user who created the asset.

updater.description: Output only. The user who last updated the asset.

createTime.description: Output only. Timestamp when the asset was created.

updateTime.description: Output only. Timestamp when the asset was last modified.

deleteTime.description: Output only. The time at which the asset was soft-deleted.

purgeTime.description: Output only. The time at which the asset will be permanently purged.

schema.title: Required. The asset with updated fields.

parameters.name: validateOnly
parameters.description: Optional. If set to true, validate only without persisting.

get.summary: Lists all versions of an asset.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Optional. The maximum number of versions to return.

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description: Optional. A pagination token from a previous call.

post.summary:
Creates a new version of an existing asset. Runs the same ingestion
pipeline as CreateAsset. The new version becomes the latest version.
Previous versions remain accessible.

parameters.name: assetVersion
parameters.description: Required. The version to create. Only change_note is user-settable.

parameters.name: filename
parameters.description:
Optional. The original filename from the client. Set for upload
versions. If omitted with no crop, this is a revert.

parameters.name: sizeBytes
parameters.description: Optional. The file size in bytes. Required for multipart uploads.

parameters.name: sourceVersion
parameters.description:
Optional. The version to derive from. Defaults to the latest
version. Used for edit and revert modes.

parameters.name: crop.area.x
parameters.description: Required. Origin X in pixels.

parameters.name: crop.area.y
parameters.description: Required. Origin Y in pixels.

parameters.name: crop.area.width
parameters.description: Required. Width in pixels. Must be positive.

parameters.name: crop.area.height
parameters.description: Required. Height in pixels. Must be positive.

parameters.name: crop.straighten
parameters.description: Optional. Straighten angle in degrees (-45 to 45).

parameters.name: crop.flipHorizontal
parameters.description: Optional. Flip horizontally.

parameters.name: crop.flipVertical
parameters.description: Optional. Flip vertically.

get.summary: Retrieves a specific version of an asset.

post.summary:
Imports assets from a storage endpoint path. Recursively scans
the path, creates asset resources, and runs the ingestion pipeline
for each discovered file. Returns an LRO that tracks overall
import progress.
post.description:
The caller must have `assets.assets.create` permission on the
parent project and `storage.endpoints.get` on the source endpoint.

AssetMediaType.description:
The broad media category of the asset.

 - MEDIA_TYPE_UNSPECIFIED: Unspecified media type.
 - IMAGE: Still image (JPEG, PNG, TIFF, WebP, etc.).
 - VIDEO: Video file (MP4, MOV, MXF, ProRes, etc.).
 - AUDIO: Audio file (WAV, MP3, AAC, FLAC, etc.).
 - GRAPHIC: Motion graphics or vector (SVG, Lottie, Rive, etc.).
 - DOCUMENT: Document (PDF, PSD, AI, etc.).

endpoint.title:
Required. The storage endpoint to scan. Format:
`organizations/{organization}/storageGateways/{gateway}/endpoints/{endpoint}`

pathPrefix.description:
Optional. The path prefix to scan within the endpoint. If empty,
scans the entire endpoint.

preservePaths.description:
Optional. If true, preserve the filesystem directory structure
as the asset path field.

autoTag.description:
Optional. If true, run AI auto-tagging on imported assets.
Defaults to true.

AssetsImportAssetsBody.description: The request sent to the ImportAssets method.

etag.description: Optional. The etag for optimistic concurrency.

AssetsUndeleteAssetBody.description: The request sent to the UndeleteAsset method.

name.description:
The server-assigned name, which is only unique within the same service that
originally returns it. If you use the default HTTP mapping, the
`name` should be a resource name ending with `operations/{unique_id}`.

metadata.description:
Service-specific metadata associated with the operation.  It typically
contains progress information and common metadata such as create time.
Some services might not provide such metadata.  Any method that returns a
long-running operation should document the metadata type, if any.

done.description:
If the value is `false`, it means the operation is still in progress.
If `true`, the operation is completed, and either `error` or `response` is
available.

error.description: The error result of the operation in case of failure or cancellation.

response.description:
The normal, successful response of the operation.  If the original
method returns no data on success, such as `Delete`, the response is
`google.protobuf.Empty`.  If the original method is standard
`Get`/`Create`/`Update`, the response should be the resource.  For other
methods, the response should have the type `XxxResponse`, where `Xxx`
is the original method name.  For example, if the original method name
is `TakeSnapshot()`, the inferred response type is
`TakeSnapshotResponse`.

longrunningOperation.description:
This resource represents a long-running operation that is the result of a
network API call.

@type.description:
A URL/resource name that uniquely identifies the type of the serialized
protocol buffer message. This string must contain at least
one "/" character. The last segment of the URL's path must represent
the fully qualified name of the type (as in
`path/google.protobuf.Duration`). The name should be in a canonical form
(e.g., leading "." is not accepted).

In practice, teams usually precompile into the binary all types that they
expect it to use in the context of Any. However, for URLs which use the
scheme `http`, `https`, or no scheme, one can optionally set up a type
server that maps type URLs to message definitions as follows:

* If no scheme is provided, `https` is assumed.
* An HTTP GET on the URL must yield a [google.protobuf.Type][]
  value in binary format, or produce an error.
* Applications are allowed to cache lookup results based on the
  URL, or have them precompiled into a binary to avoid any
  lookup. Therefore, binary compatibility needs to be preserved
  on changes to types. (Use versioned type names to manage
  breaking changes.)

Note: this functionality is not currently available in the official
protobuf release, and it is not used for type URLs beginning with
type.googleapis.com. As of May 2023, there are no widely used type server
implementations and no plans to implement one.

Schemes other than `http`, `https` (or the empty scheme) might be
used with implementation specific semantics.

protobufNullValue.description:
`NullValue` is a singleton enumeration to represent the null value for the
`Value` type union.

The JSON representation for `NullValue` is JSON `null`.

 - NULL_VALUE: Null value.

code.description:
The status code, which should be an enum value of
[google.rpc.Code][google.rpc.Code].

message.description:
A developer-facing error message, which should be in English. Any
user-facing error message should be localized and sent in the
[google.rpc.Status.details][google.rpc.Status.details] field, or localized
by the client.

details.description:
A list of messages that carry the error details.  There is a common set of
message types for APIs to use.

rpcStatus.description:
The `Status` type defines a logical error model that is suitable for
different programming environments, including REST APIs and RPC APIs. It is
used by [gRPC](https://github.com/grpc). Each `Status` message contains
three pieces of data: error code, error message, and error details.

You can find out more about this error model and how to work with it in the
[API Design Guide](https://cloud.google.com/apis/design/errors).

name.title:
Output only. The resource name of the asset. Format:
`organizations/{organization}/projects/{project}/assets/{asset}`

displayName.description: Required. A human-readable name for the asset.

state.description: Output only. The current lifecycle state.

mediaType.description:
Output only. The broad media category, determined during ingestion
from the file's MIME type.

mimeType.description:
Output only. The MIME type of the original file (e.g. `image/jpeg`,
`video/mp4`). Determined from magic bytes during ingestion, not
from the file extension.

path.description:
Optional. An organizational path for display grouping (e.g.
`/sports/highlights/2026/`). Not enforced as a hierarchy — purely
for UI presentation and filesystem import preservation. Use tags
for structured categorization.

endpoint.title:
Output only. The storage endpoint where the asset's files are stored.
Format: `organizations/{organization}/storageGateways/{gateway}/endpoints/{endpoint}`

checksumSha256.description:
Output only. SHA-256 checksum of the original file. Used for
deduplication — if a file with the same checksum already exists
in the project, the existing asset is returned instead of creating
a duplicate.

sizeBytes.description: Output only. Size of the original file in bytes.

latestVersion.description: Output only. The latest version of the asset.

versionCount.description: Output only. Total number of versions.

technicalMetadata.description:
Output only. Technical metadata extracted from the file (EXIF, XMP).
Junk metadata (Adobe comments, software tags) is stripped.
Stored as a structured JSON object — fields vary by media type.

aiDescription.description:
Output only. AI-generated description of the asset's visual or
audio content. Used for semantic search.

transcription.description:
Output only. Transcription of audio/video content. Indexed for
full-text search.

duration.description: Output only. Duration of the media, for video and audio assets.

width.description: Output only. Width in pixels, for image and video assets.

height.description: Output only. Height in pixels, for image and video assets.

expireTime.description:
Optional. The time at which this asset should expire. When set,
a background reaper will soft-delete the asset after this time.
Can be set directly or via a retention policy (tag-based rules).

ttl.description:
Input only. The time-to-live for the asset, as an alternative to
setting expire_time directly. The server computes expire_time from
create_time + ttl.

annotations.description: Optional. Annotations associated with this asset.

etag.description:
Output only. A checksum computed by the server based on the current
value of the Asset resource.

creator.description: Output only. The user who created the asset.

updater.description: Output only. The user who last updated the asset.

createTime.description: Output only. Timestamp when the asset was created.

updateTime.description: Output only. Timestamp when the asset was last modified.

deleteTime.description: Output only. The time at which the asset was soft-deleted.

purgeTime.description: Output only. The time at which the asset will be permanently purged.

v1Asset.description:
A media asset. Assets are immutable — file content cannot be changed
after creation. Use versions for new iterations of the same logical
asset. Assets are flat (no folder hierarchy); use tags for organization
and the optional path field for display grouping.

v1AssetState.description:
Asset lifecycle states.

 - STATE_UNSPECIFIED: Unspecified state.
 - PLACEHOLDER: The asset is a placeholder with no file content. Created by
the Request workflow — the file will be provided when an
artist fulfills the associated line item.
 - PROCESSING: The asset is being ingested (upload, metadata extraction,
thumbnail generation, AI processing).
 - ACTIVE: The asset is fully ingested and available.
 - FAILED: An ingestion step failed. Check the latest version's
ingestion_error for details.
 - DELETE_REQUESTED: The asset has been marked for deletion. Can be restored
with UndeleteAsset before purge_time.

name.title:
Output only. The resource name of the version. Format:
`organizations/{organization}/projects/{project}/assets/{asset}/versions/{version}`

versionNumber.description: Output only. The sequential version number (1, 2, 3...).

checksumSha256.description:
Output only. SHA-256 checksum of this version's file. Only set
for upload versions (not edit versions).

sizeBytes.description:
Output only. Size of this version's file in bytes. Only set
for upload versions.

mimeType.description: Output only. MIME type of this version's file.

storageKey.description:
Output only. The storage object key for the file. Only set for
upload versions. Edit versions derive their output from the
source version's blob + the crop operations.

renditions.description: Output only. Generated renditions (thumbnails, proxies, previews).

changeNote.description: Optional. A note describing what changed in this version.

ingestionError.description: Output only. If the ingestion pipeline failed, the error details.

creator.description: Output only. The user who created this version.

createTime.description: Output only. Timestamp when this version was created.

sourceVersion.description:
Output only. The version this was derived from. Empty for upload
versions. Set for edit versions (crop) and reverts.

crop.description:
Output only. The crop/edit applied to the source version.
Empty for upload versions.

v1AssetVersion.description:
A specific version of an asset. A version is either an upload
(has a blob) or an edit (has a crop + pointer to the source version).
Versions are immutable once created. The asset's latest_version
field always points to the most recent version.

area.description:
Optional. The crop area to apply. If set, width and height must
be positive.

straighten.description: Optional. Straighten angle in degrees (-45 to 45).

v1Crop.description: Crop operation combining crop area, straighten, and flip.

width.description: Required. Width in pixels. Must be positive.

height.description: Required. Height in pixels. Must be positive.

v1CropArea.description: The crop area within an image.

versions.description: The versions of the specified asset, ordered by version_number descending.

nextPageToken.description: A pagination token for the next page.

v1ListAssetVersionsResponse.description: The response from the ListAssetVersions method.

assets.description: The assets belonging to the specified project.

nextPageToken.description: A pagination token for the next page.

v1ListAssetsResponse.description: The response from the ListAssets method.

storageKey.description: Output only. The storage object key for this rendition.

mimeType.description: Output only. MIME type of the rendition.

width.description: Output only. Width in pixels (for image/video renditions).

height.description: Output only. Height in pixels (for image/video renditions).

sizeBytes.description: Output only. Size of the rendition in bytes.

v1Rendition.description: A generated rendition of an asset version (thumbnail, proxy, preview).

v1RenditionType.description:
The type of rendition.

 - TYPE_UNSPECIFIED: Unspecified rendition type.
 - THUMBNAIL_SMALL: Small thumbnail (e.g. 150x150).
 - THUMBNAIL_MEDIUM: Medium thumbnail (e.g. 400x400).
 - THUMBNAIL_LARGE: Large thumbnail (e.g. 800x800).
 - ANIMATED_PREVIEW: Animated preview for video (short WebP/GIF loop).
 - VIDEO_PROXY: Video proxy for web playback (H.264 720p).
 - AUDIO_PREVIEW: Audio preview (MP3, short clip or full).
 - POSTER_FRAME: Poster frame extracted from video.
